fundamentals.name: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. engages in the design, manufacture, and sale of fiber-optic networking products in the United States, Taiwan, and China. The company offers optical modules, optical filters, lasers, laser components, subassemblies, transmitters and transceivers, turn-key equipment, headend, node, and distribution equipment, as well as amplifiers. It sells its products to internet data center operators, cable television, telecom equipment manufacturers, fiber-to-the-home, and internet service providers through its direct and indirect sales channels. Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. was incorporated in 1997 and is headquartered in Sugar Land, Texas.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue and strong analyst estimates are offset by current unprofitability and a high valuation.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates (-0.07 vs -0.05); next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 51.4% YoY — indicating strong top-line expansion.
Analyst growth estimates are very high for current Q (109.4%) and FY (441.7%).
PEG ratio 0.78 — suggests growth may be outpacing its forward P/E.
Strong liquidity with current ratio 3.83 and quick ratio 2.85.
negatives: Operating margin -8.6% and profit margin -8.5% — the company is currently unprofitable.
Return on equity -6.1% and return on assets -3.3% — indicates poor returns on capital.
Free cash flow (TTM) is negative $-445.58M — burning cash significantly.
Forward P/E of 38.03 and Price/Sales of 28.72 are high, especially given current losses.

news.paragraph: Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) has seen significant positive attention, with a recent article highlighting its 635.5% gain over the past six months and how to play the stock. This surge comes amid broader market interest in AI-related companies, though some other AI leaders experienced sell-offs on May 19th. The company's next earnings date is August 6th, which may serve as a near-term catalyst.
